name: consistency-bugs-long-story-generation
description: Detect and categorize consistency errors in long-form LLM story generation using entropy-based signals and positional analysis. Apply to evaluate and improve narrative coherence in 8,000-10,000 word stories through automated contradiction detection across timeline, character, world-building, and factual dimensions.
paperTitle: Lost in Stories: Consistency Bugs in Long Story Generation by LLMs
content:
## Problem

Large language models frequently contradict established facts, character traits, and world rules in long stories despite generating coherent text locally. Traditional evaluation focuses on local quality but misses global consistency violations that degrade narrative quality across 10,000+ word outputs.

## Actionable Technique: Entropy-Based Consistency Detection

Monitor token-level entropy as an early-warning signal for inconsistency. Error content exhibits 12-19% higher entropy than baseline text, enabling predictive identification of problematic regions before full contradiction analysis.

```python
# Calculate token-level entropy for consistency warnings
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import entropy as scipy_entropy

def compute_token_entropy(logits):
    """Compute per-token entropy from language model logits."""
    probs = np.exp(logits) / np.sum(np.exp(logits))
    return scipy_entropy(probs)

def flag_high_entropy_regions(token_logits, threshold=0.85):
    """Identify high-entropy tokens indicating uncertainty/errors."""
    entropies = [compute_token_entropy(logit) for logit in token_logits]
    return [i for i, e in enumerate(entropies) if e > threshold]
```

This enables focusing detailed consistency analysis on regions where the model shows highest uncertainty.

## Actionable Technique: Positional Error Concentration

Target consistency checks around mid-narrative sections (40-60% of story length) where error concentration peaks. This 20-percentage-point band encompasses 40% of all consistency errors—the highest density region—allowing efficient resource allocation.

## Actionable Technique: Error Correlation Mapping

Recognize that factual inconsistencies correlate strongly with characterization failures. Design mitigation strategies addressing root causes rather than symptoms by:
- Analyzing co-occurrence patterns between error types
- Prioritizing fixes for high-correlation pairs
- Using characterization coherence as proxy for factual consistency

## When NOT to use

- Local text generation tasks without long-range dependencies
- Domain-specific stories requiring expert validation (medical, legal narratives may need human-in-the-loop)
- Real-time applications where entropy computation adds unacceptable latency

## Error Taxonomy Reference

The ConStory-Bench taxonomy identifies five primary categories:
1. Timeline & Plot Logic (temporal contradictions, plot holes)
2. Characterization (trait/personality inconsistencies)
3. World-building & Setting (location and rule violations)
4. Factual & Detail Consistency (specific fact contradictions)
5. Narrative & Style (tonal/voice shifts)

Each category contains 3-4 subtypes for fine-grained diagnostics.

## References

Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.05890
